root_text.sn38.15:1.3: “Pañcime, āvuso, upādānakkhandhā sakkāyo vutto bhagavatā, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn38.15:1.5: Ime kho, āvuso, pañcupādānakkhandhā sakkāyo vutto bhagavatā”ti.
root_text.sn38.15:1.6: “Atthi panāvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā, etassa sakkāyassa pariññāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.15:1.7: “Atthi kho, āvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā, etassa sakkāyassa pariññāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.15:1.8: “Katamo panāvuso, maggo katamā paṭipadā, etassa sakkāyassa pariññāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.15:1.9: “Ayameva kho, āvuso, ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo, etassa sakkāyassa pariññāya, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn38.15:1.11: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, maggo ayaṁ paṭipadā, etassa sakkāyassa pariññāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.15:1.12: “Bhaddako, āvuso, maggo bhaddikā paṭipadā, etassa sakkāyassa pariññāya.

translation_text.sn38.15:1.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘substantial reality’.
translation_text.sn38.15:1.3: “Reverend, the Buddha said that these five grasping aggregates are substantial reality. That is,
translation_text.sn38.15:1.4: form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.
translation_text.sn38.15:1.5: The Buddha said that these five grasping aggregates are substantial reality.”
translation_text.sn38.15:1.6: “But, reverend, is there a path and a practice for completely understanding this substantial reality?”
